feed.title: Academic Affairs Notes
feed.subtitle: Updates from the Student Association Academic Affairs Committee

entry.title: Three priorities for the fall
entry.summary: Where syllabus access, exam relief, and the spring forum each stand this term.
entry.content: Three priorities are moving this fall. Syllabus access asks that course outlines post at least a week before registration opens. Exam relief gathers registrar data on three finals in one day. And planning has started for a spring forum on advising. Stop by the academic affairs desk to help shape any of them.

entry.title: Syllabus access draft heads to the faculty senate
entry.summary: The recommendation language is refined and ready for committee review.
entry.content: Students keep telling us they pick classes blind. The draft recommendation asks that syllabi post at least one week before registration opens, so workload and books are known before a student commits. The committee refined the language this week and the draft goes to the faculty senate this term.

entry.title: Exam conflict data requested from the registrar
entry.summary: We are counting how often three finals land in a single day before proposing a policy.
entry.content: Three finals in one day is more common than it should be. Rather than just complain, we asked the registrar for the numbers by college. Once the counts are in, the committee will propose a fair conflict policy.

entry.title: Save the date, an advising town hall
entry.summary: Bring questions about advising, syllabus access, and exam scheduling.
entry.content: An academic affairs town hall is set for October 22, 2026 in Goldstein Auditorium from 5:30 to 7:00 PM. Members of the committee will be there to listen and take ideas back to the faculty senate. No registration needed, just show up.

entry.title: Planning opens for the spring forum on advising
entry.summary: The first planning meeting is set for October 26, 2026.
entry.content: Planning has started for a spring event on advising and course planning. The first planning meeting is October 26, 2026, with the room being confirmed. If you want to help shape it, the committee meets every other Monday and visitors are welcome.
